history: The new ৳5 banknote was introduced by the Government of Bangladesh in 2026 as part of Bangladesh's new currency series based on the theme Historical and Archaeological Architecture of Bangladesh. The note features the Star Mosque (Tara Masjid) and the national flower, the water lily, on the front, while the back displays Graffiti-2024 commemorating the 2024 mass uprising. It also includes improved security features such as a Royal Bengal Tiger watermark and an embedded security thread. The new note circulates alongside older ৳5 notes and coins.
economy: Issued during 2025–2026, the banknote reflects Bangladesh's efforts to stabilize inflation, improve financial governance, strengthen the banking sector, and maintain cash circulation alongside the rapid expansion of digital payment services.
security: Advanced security features include a Royal Bengal Tiger watermark, embedded security thread, microprinting, see-through register, latent image, ultraviolet fluorescent elements, tactile features for the visually impaired, and high-quality anti-counterfeiting printing technology.

history: The redesigned Bangladesh 10 Taka note, introduced as part of the 2025–2026 currency series, reflects Bangladesh's history and cultural heritage. The front features the Baitul Mukarram National Mosque with the national flower (water lily), while the reverse displays Graffiti-2024, symbolizing the July 2024 movement. The note also includes enhanced security features such as a tiger watermark, color-changing security thread, and microprinting to reduce counterfeiting. Existing Tk 10 notes remain valid alongside the new design.
economy: Issued during 2025–2026, the banknote reflects Bangladesh's efforts to stabilize inflation, improve financial governance, strengthen the banking sector, and maintain cash circulation alongside the rapid expansion of digital payment services.
security: Advanced security features include a Royal Bengal Tiger watermark, embedded security thread, microprinting, see-through register, latent image, ultraviolet fluorescent elements, tactile features for the visually impaired, and high-quality anti-counterfeiting printing technology.

history: The new 20 Taka banknote was introduced by Bangladesh Bank on 1 June 2025 as part of a new series highlighting Bangladesh's historical and archaeological heritage. Unlike previous notes, it does not feature the portrait of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman. Instead, the front displays the Kantajew Temple in Dinajpur, while the back features the Somapura Mahavihara in Naogaon. The redesigned note includes enhanced security features and reflects a broader effort to modernize Bangladesh's currency and showcase the nation's rich cultural heritage.
economy: Issued during 2025–2026, the banknote reflects Bangladesh's efforts to stabilize inflation, improve financial governance, strengthen the banking sector, and maintain cash circulation alongside the rapid expansion of digital payment services.
security: Advanced security features include a Royal Bengal Tiger watermark, embedded security thread, microprinting, see-through register, latent image, ultraviolet fluorescent elements, tactile features for the visually impaired, and high-quality anti-counterfeiting printing technology.

history: The redesigned Bangladesh 50 Taka note, first issued on 1 June 2025, is part of the new currency series highlighting Bangladesh's historical and archaeological heritage. The front features Ahsan Manzil (the Pink Palace in Dhaka) with the national flower, the water lily, while the reverse displays 'Sangram' (The Struggle), a famous painting by Zainul Abedin. The note does not include any human portrait and incorporates advanced security features such as a tiger watermark, security thread, and microprinting to prevent counterfeiting. Existing Tk 50 notes remain legal tender alongside the new design
economy: Issued during 2025–2026, the banknote reflects Bangladesh's efforts to stabilize inflation, improve financial governance, strengthen the banking sector, and maintain cash circulation alongside the rapid expansion of digital payment services.
security: Advanced security features include a Royal Bengal Tiger watermark, embedded security thread, microprinting, see-through register, latent image, ultraviolet fluorescent elements, tactile features for the visually impaired, and high-quality anti-counterfeiting printing technology.

history: The redesigned Bangladesh 100 Taka note, introduced on 12 August 2025, is part of the new currency series celebrating Bangladesh's historical and archaeological heritage. The front features the Sixty Dome Mosque (Shat Gombuj Mosque) in Bagerhat with the national flower, the water lily, while the reverse depicts the Sundarbans, symbolizing the country's natural heritage. The note includes advanced security features such as a Royal Bengal Tiger watermark, a color-changing security thread, microprinting, and tactile marks for the visually impaired. Existing Tk 100 notes continue to remain legal tender alongside the new design
economy: Issued during 2025–2026, the banknote reflects Bangladesh's efforts to stabilize inflation, improve financial governance, strengthen the banking sector, and maintain cash circulation alongside the rapid expansion of digital payment services.
security: Advanced security features include a Royal Bengal Tiger watermark, embedded security thread, microprinting, see-through register, latent image, ultraviolet fluorescent elements, tactile features for the visually impaired, and high-quality anti-counterfeiting printing technology.

history: The redesigned Bangladesh 500 Taka note, introduced on 4 December 2025, is part of the new Historical and Archaeological Architecture of Bangladesh currency series. The front features the Central Shaheed Minar with the national flower (water lily), while the reverse displays the Bangladesh Supreme Court building. The note includes advanced security features such as a Royal Bengal Tiger watermark, color-changing ink, a security thread, microprinting, and tactile marks for visually impaired users. Older Tk 500 notes remain legal tender alongside the new design
economy: Issued during 2025–2026, the banknote reflects Bangladesh's efforts to stabilize inflation, improve financial governance, strengthen the banking sector, and maintain cash circulation alongside the rapid expansion of digital payment services.
security: Advanced security features include a Royal Bengal Tiger watermark, embedded security thread, microprinting, see-through register, latent image, ultraviolet fluorescent elements, tactile features for the visually impaired, and high-quality anti-counterfeiting printing technology.

history: The redesigned Bangladesh 1,000 Taka note, first issued on 1 June 2025, is part of the new Historical and Archaeological Architecture of Bangladesh currency series. The front features the National Martyrs' Memorial (Jatiyo Smriti Soudho) in Savar with the national flower (water lily), while the reverse displays the Jatiya Sangsad Bhaban (National Parliament Building) in Dhaka. The note includes advanced security features such as a Royal Bengal Tiger watermark, a color-changing security thread, microprinting, and tactile marks for visually impaired users. Existing Tk 1,000 notes remain legal tender alongside the new design.
economy: Issued during 2025–2026, the banknote reflects Bangladesh's efforts to stabilize inflation, improve financial governance, strengthen the banking sector, and maintain cash circulation alongside the rapid expansion of digital payment services.
security: Advanced security features include a Royal Bengal Tiger watermark, embedded security thread, microprinting, see-through register, latent image, ultraviolet fluorescent elements, tactile features for the visually impaired, and high-quality anti-counterfeiting printing technology.
